subject: feat(toast): Phase 2 — migrate Toast to DaisyUI toast + alert (unshadow .toast-*)
body:
Second of 10 phases from the DaisyUI component-class audit. This phase
migrates the toast notification system to DaisyUI's `toast` positioning
wrapper + `alert alert-*` semantic variants, unshadowing the custom
.toast-* classes that were defined in styles.css and silently
overriding DaisyUI's built-in toast component.

Changes:

Toast.jsx — ToastItem now renders as `<div className="alert alert-${type}
shadow-lg transition-all duration-200 ease-out ...">` with the
type-to-class mapping in a small ALERT_CLASS_BY_TYPE object (success ->
alert-success, error -> alert-error, warning -> alert-warning, info ->
alert-info). Enter/exit animations are now Tailwind transition utilities
(opacity-0 translate-y-2 while exiting, opacity-100 translate-y-0
otherwise) instead of custom .toast-enter/.toast-exit keyframes. The
dismiss button is now `btn btn-ghost btn-xs btn-circle`. ToastContainer
uses `toast toast-bottom toast-center` (DaisyUI's positioning wrapper
classes) with an inline `style={{ zIndex: 'var(--z-toast)' }}` to pin
the stacking above other dashboard layers (DaisyUI's default z-index
doesn't stack above our debug pill or drawers).

styles.css — deleted ~65 lines of custom .toast-* CSS:
  - .toast-container (positioning)
  - .toast-item (layout, transitions)
  - .toast-enter / .toast-exit (animation states)
  - .toast-message
  - .toast-dismiss / .toast-dismiss:hover
  - .toast-success / .toast-error / .toast-info / .toast-warning
    (type colors that duplicated DaisyUI alert-* exactly)

All replaced with a one-line comment pointing at the component file.

Why this fixes the shadowing:

Our styles.css used to define .toast-container, .toast-item, etc.
UNLAYERED, which means they won ALL priority contests against DaisyUI's
own `.toast` / `.alert` definitions inside `@layer components`. Any
future `className="toast toast-top"` or `className="alert alert-error"`
usage elsewhere in the codebase would silently get our custom styles
instead of DaisyUI's component behavior. Deleting the custom classes
means DaisyUI's real `.toast` and `.alert` classes now apply, which is
what we actually want.

What's preserved:

- useToast() hook API and addToast/removeToast signatures — unchanged
- Auto-dismiss timer semantics (duration=0 means persistent)
- Two-stage isExiting state for animation-before-unmount ordering
- ARIA role="alert" for errors / role="status" for non-errors
- aria-live="assertive"/"polite" for screen reader urgency
- no-print class so toasts don't leak into PDF exports
- z-index: var(--z-toast) (= 70 from our scale, above drawers and menus)

Build verification:
  - npm test: 21 passing (no test regression)
  - npm run build: passes, 84 modules
  - DaisyUI .toast, .toast-bottom, .toast-center, .alert, .alert-success,
    .alert-error, .alert-warning, .alert-info, .alert-soft all present
    in built CSS
  - Old .toast-item / .toast-dismiss / .toast-message / .toast-enter /
    .toast-exit confirmed GONE from built CSS
  - Toast.jsx useToast API still referenced in the JS bundle

Tags: ui, daisyui, refactor, a11y, toast
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-component-migration
Semver: patch
